mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, driving positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production reflects supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing output, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and yields negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines fail to predict negative forward returns, with a 20-day return frequency above 55% during contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers immediate expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months where CPI inflation exceeds the 0.4 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and generating positive 60-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction effect shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly translates to higher aggregate corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to predict positive forward returns, with a 20-day return frequency below 45% during expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of tight monetary policy signals stagflationary pressure, forcing immediate equity deleveraging and negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency drops below 40% during tightening periods when industrial production declines, invalidating the stagflation transmission channel.
